title: AT&T Inc. (T) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of AT&T Inc. (T). Economic Prospect Score: 44/100 — Weak Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: AT&T Inc. is projected to grow FCF at 3% annually, reflecting its competitive position, sector dynamics, and historical compounding rate.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 8.5% WACC balances AT&T Inc.'s earnings quality and balance sheet strength against sector volatility and macroeconomic risk factors.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.0% terminal rate assumes long-run growth converges toward nominal GDP, appropriate for a mature S&P 500 constituent.

thesisParagraphs: AT&T Inc. (T) is a large-cap constituent of the S&P 500 with established market position and consistent cash flow generation. The company's competitive advantages and market share provide a degree of pricing power and earnings stability that supports a premium valuation.

My DCF applies a 3% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability and forward outlook, with a 8.5% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks and capital market conditions. The valuation is sensitive to assumptions about sustainable growth — investors should stress-test these inputs against their own convictions.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is AT&T Inc.'s intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 3% FCF growth and 8.5% WACC, Opus calculates T's intrinsic value at approximately $37.71 per share, suggesting the stock is undervalued at $28.00.

faq.question: What assumptions drive T's valuation?
faq.answer: The key drivers are the FCF growth rate (3%) and discount rate (8.5%). A 1% increase in growth adds roughly 10-15% to the intrinsic value. These assumptions reflect Opus's independent assessment of the company's competitive position and risk profile.

faq.question: Should I buy or sell based on this analysis?
faq.answer: This is not financial advice. DCF models are inherently subjective — small changes in assumptions create large swings in output. Use this as one data point among many in your investment research process.

prospectVerdictDetail: AT&T is a turnaround story — divested WarnerMedia, cut the dividend, and is focused on fiber and 5G. Fiber subscriber growth is strong. But the company carries massive debt ($130B+), wireless competition is intense, and decades of shareholder value destruction have eroded trust.
prospectKeyRisks: Massive debt load ($130B+) constraining investment capacity and creating refinancing risk if rates stay elevated
Wireless ARPU pressure from T-Mobile and Verizon competing aggressively on pricing and bundling
Fixed wireless broadband (T-Mobile) cannibalizing AT&T's fiber growth in overlapping markets
prospectKeyCatalysts: Fiber-to-the-home subscriber growth continuing at 1M+/year, building a high-quality broadband base
Debt reduction reaching $100B target, enabling credit rating upgrades and lower interest expenses
5G enterprise solutions (private networks, edge computing) opening a new revenue stream beyond consumer wireless
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100. Each pillar is broken into individually scored factors with transparent rationale. Data sources include FY2025 10-K filings, analyst consensus estimates, news sentiment analysis, and competitive landscape assessment. The score is forward-looking and represents economic prospect over a 2-3 year horizon.
